author: D. Fox
source: computed by H4Core.symOf and (toString p).length; NOT hardcoded
honest_findings: On this 6-point 13-digit sample, symOf = 1 for every p > 10^12, and every p is far below 3^40 (~1.216e19).
10^12 < 3^40 by ~7 orders of magnitude; as a collapse horizon, 3^40 sits far above where the sample collapse is first seen.
SAMPLE observation only: NOT a proof that every p > 10^12 has Sym = 1, and NOT a proof that 10^12 is a universal boundary.
'TimeHorizon'/3^40 carries no dynamical or temporal meaning here; it is just an integer the witnesses are compared against.
